Title: The Zma-miRNA319-ZmMYB74 Module Regulates Maize Resistance to Stalk Rot Disease by Modulating Lignin Deposition

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls, restricts fungal invasion, contributes to stalk rot resistance and stalk strength
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots; stalk vascular cylinder; exodermal/hypodermal cell walls
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates maize resistance to stalk rot by suppressing ZmMYB74/138, thereby favoring lignin deposition; overexpression reduced lignin after infection while improving resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. zma-miR319 was induced by Fusarium graminearum and Pythium inflatum infection. 2. STTM-mediated silencing of zma-miR319 increased ZmMYB74 and ZmMYB138 expression and enhanced disease susceptibility. 3. SCMV-mediated overexpression of zma-miR319 reduced ZmMYB74 and ZmMYB138 transcript abundance, reduced lignin content after infection, and inhibited pathogen infection, supporting a positive role in resistance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: sRNA-seq; degradome-seq target identification; Northern blot; STTM virus-based silencing; SCMV-mediated overexpression; RT-qPCR; disease severity index phenotyping; lignin quantification
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in leaves and also in some root comparisons after Fg/Pi infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in infected leaves of both genotypes and in certain infected roots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum infection; Pythium inflatum infection
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduced lignin content after pathogen inoculation; potential impact on structural traits not directly resolved for miR319 in this paper
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be considered for engineering pathogen-responsive resistance modules, but reduced lignin cautions against unrestricted constitutive deployment
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SCMV vector-mediated overexpression; STTM virus-based suppression
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: sRNA-seq PRJNA843944; degradome-seq PRJNA843942; transcriptome RNA-seq PRJNA843784
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although miR319 overexpression reduced lignin, it still improved resistance, likely because the dominant effect is repression of the negative regulator ZmMYB74; the paper centers final mechanistic resolution on the miR319-ZmMYB74-ZmCAD module.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly supplies immediate monolignol precursors for lignin deposition
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Positive regulator of lignin-associated disease resistance; key downstream target of ZmMYB74 in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall reinforcement, reduced fungal spread, increased stalk rot resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root exodermis/hypodermis and vascular cylinder; stalk cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin accumulation and disease resistance; its repression by ZmMYB74 lowers lignin and increases susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCAD silencing facilitated infection by Fg and Pi and reduced root lignin content. 2. ZmMYB74 directly bound AC elements in the ZmCAD promoter in vitro and in vivo. 3. ZmMYB74 strongly repressed ZmCAD promoter activity, establishing ZmCAD as the direct metabolic effector underlying lignin-mediated resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BMV-VIGS; RT-qPCR; disease phenotyping; lignin quantification; DAP-seq; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function assayed in infected maize seedlings/roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum infection; Pythium inflatum infection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces lignin and increases susceptibility; likely weaker structural integrity though not independently quantified for ZmCAD lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Positive target for improving lignin-mediated stalk rot resistance, especially in combination with reducing ZmMYB74 repression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq peaks for ZmMYB74 binding; transcriptome RNA-seq PRJNA843784
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the direct transcriptional target mediating the ZmMYB74 effect on lignin deposition.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall reinforcement and pathogen resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports lignin accumulation and resistance; silencing increased susceptibility and lowered lignin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BMV-VIGS silencing of ZmPAL1 increased susceptibility to stalk rot pathogens. 2. Silencing lowered lignin content in infected roots. 3. These results support a protective role of upstream phenylpropanoid flux in lignin-mediated defence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BMV-VIGS; RT-qPCR; disease phenotyping; lignin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum infection; Pythium inflatum infection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing compromises defence; broader metabolic perturbation possible but not detailed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential positive engineering target to reinforce lignin-mediated defence, though upstream pathway manipulation may have pleiotropic effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as supporting lignin-pathway evidence rather than a central mechanistic target.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies monolignol precursor intermediates for lignin production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall strengthening and pathogen defence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin deposition and resistance; silencing increased fungal infection and lowered lignin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BMV-VIGS silencing of ZmCCoAOMT2 promoted stalk rot pathogen infection. 2. Silencing decreased root lignin content. 3. This supports a positive role for monolignol biosynthesis in resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BMV-VIGS; RT-qPCR; disease phenotyping; lignin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum infection; Pythium inflatum infection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing compromises defence; broader wall composition effects not detailed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate positive target to support lignin-mediated resistance, potentially in pathway stacking
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive pathway gene; not shown as direct ZmMYB74 target in ChIP assays.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor; GA-inducible MYB clade
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Lignin biosynthesis via transcriptional repression of monolignol pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses ZmCAD promoter activity by binding AC-I/AC-IV-related elements
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: zma-miR319; Fusarium graminearum infection; Pythium inflatum infection; GA3; promoter haplotype In/Del variation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator of defence-induced lignin deposition and stalk rot resistance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall reinforcement; restriction of fungal colonization; contribution to stalk strength
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots and stalk tissues, especially exodermal/hypodermal and vascular tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates lignin deposition and maize resistance to stalk rot; higher ZmMYB74 increases susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMYB74 was identified by degradome, transcriptome, and WGCNA as a zma-miR319 target and hub regulator responsive to stalk rot pathogens. 2. Silencing/knockout of ZmMYB74 enhanced resistance, while transgenic enhanced expression increased susceptibility in seedlings and field-tested adult plants. 3. ZmMYB74 directly binds the ZmCAD promoter and represses its transcription, reducing lignin deposition. 4. Natural promoter In/Dels lowered ZmMYB74 transcription and were associated with enhanced resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: degradome-seq; WGCNA; RT-qPCR; GA3 induction; BMV-VIGS; Mu insertion mutant analysis; stable transgenic enhanced-expression and RNAi lines; disease phenotyping; WGA fungal staining; TEM; lignin staining/quantification; DAP-seq; TF-centered Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; promoter haplotype association and reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by GA3; differentially responsive to pathogen infection; higher induction in susceptible haplotype/group after infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Responsive in infected roots; hub gene in Fg-responsive module M23; transcript level affected by promoter haplotype and transgene state
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for effector/overexpression constructs; native ZmMYB74 promoter haplotypes Hap1 and Hap2 in reporter assays; Ubi promoter in ChIP material (Ubi::ZmMYB74-GFP)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum infection; Pythium inflatum infection; GA3 treatment; natural promoter In/Dels
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher expression reduces lignin deposition, increases disease susceptibility, weakens stalk strength metrics; prior work cited indicates knockout enlarges grain size and improves seed yield under infection
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value breeding target for gene editing, RNAi, or marker-assisted selection of low-expression promoter haplotypes (Hap1) to enhance stalk rot resistance and potentially improve agronomic performance under disease pressure
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: degradome-seq PRJNA843942; transcriptome RNA-seq PRJNA843784; sRNA-seq PRJNA843944; DAP-seq accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter In/Del-21 and In/Del-8 are associated with differential transcription and resistance; study positions ZmMYB74 as the core causal regulator in the module.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor; GA-inducible MYB clade
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Likely transcriptional regulation; precise mode not established here
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not decisive; tested but found less important than ZmMYB74 for stalk rot resistance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall reinforcement and pathogen defence
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No major contribution to stalk rot resistance demonstrated relative to ZmMYB74
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMYB138 is a degradome-supported zma-miR319 target and is GA3-inducible. 2. VIGS and Mu mutant analyses showed that ZmMYB138 does not contribute as strongly as ZmMYB74 to maize stalk rot resistance. 3. The study deprioritized ZmMYB138 as the main regulatory determinant of lignin-mediated resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: degradome-seq; RT-qPCR; GA3 treatment; BMV-VIGS; Mu insertion mutant analysis; disease phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed during pathogen response, with contrasting pattern to ZmMYB74 in some comparisons
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum infection; Pythium inflatum infection; GA3 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority target than ZmMYB74 for resistance engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally tested in Results, but evidence indicates it is not the principal causal regulator.
